payloads_or_poc: DOM-based XSS occurs when unsafe JavaScript code processes user-controllable data (from a DOM source) and passes it to a DOM sink. This allows attackers to craft JavaScript payloads that would be evaluated by the vulnera…

Now it's time to craft a working proof of concept that executes JavaScript in the victim's browser. To do so, your payload depends on 2 factors: 1) the context in which your input is reflected, and 2) any existing filter…
Let's take a look at several contexts in which your unsanitized input can appear, and also go through a few payloads that can help us break out of it and achieve code execution.
